backlog.hypothesis: CONCLUDED 2026-06-15 (keep). Same-channel 🔍 REVIEW prefix on status∈{NO_ANIMAL,UNCLASSIFIABLE} flags 99% of FP at 0 FN; live since 06-12, location-agnostic. Second-channel routing is OUT per Daniel (no 2nd channel — adds overhead, won't click two). See runs/0001 + LEARNINGS.
Raise SPECIES_UNKNOWN_SPECIES_THRESHOLD 0.5 -> 0.75. Replay-gated; parked until replay.py is real.

CONCLUDED 2026-06-17 (not viable in current scene). In-tick spatial diagnostic (diff burst frames → motion centroid, 70 labeled detections w/ frames on disk: 18 real-animal incl wrong_species, 52 FP). FP and animal motion centroids are spatially ENTANGLED — both center-weighted (median ~0.5/0.5). NO edge band removes FP without removing a comparable-or-greater share of animals: left15% catches 8%FP vs 17%animals (hurts animals MORE), right15% 12%FP vs 11%animals (wash), top15% 10%FP vs 11%animals (wash), bottom15% 0 animals but only 1/52 FP (negligible). No zero-observed-FN ROI exists → FN-veto stands on data, not assumption. Reinforces exp #4 (motion knobs don't separate FP from animal) + exp #1 (notification-layer REVIEW prefix is the right architecture, not trigger-side suppression). See JOURNAL 2026-06-17.

USER-FLAGGED (Daniel, 2026-06-09): many FP are recurrent near-identical static scenes that MOG2 background subtraction should have absorbed. Diagnose why repeated/static frames aren't suppressed (central-region weighting re-amplifying same edge motion, motion_threshold 500px too low vs MOG2 residual noise, learning rate / history=500 vs trigger cadence, shadow/lighting drift) before tuning. Potentially high-impact FP reduction. See JOURNAL 2026-06-09 user note.

nightly_verdict: Every one of last night's 25 triggers was a false alarm — wind-blown garden plants — including 5 the AI mislabelled as 'animal' and sent to the main channel as if real sightings; no change deployed, since there's still no safe way to filter these without risking real animals. Heads-up: two days now with no feedback labels, so one more label-free day trips the automatic safety freeze — a few taps on recent alerts would prevent it.
